paper.title: Systematic search for blue hyper-velocity stars from LAMOST survey

review.summary: This paper reports a systematic search for blue hyper-velocity stars using LAMOST DR10 low-resolution spectra combined with Gaia DR3 astrometry. It contains five internal tables: a selection summary of 124 high-velocity candidates by velocity criteria and distance reliability; a catalog of 125 high-velocity candidates with positions, MK spectral classifications, rest-frame velocities, distances, and flags; and three object-by-object parameter tables presenting detailed properties (photometry, proper motions, radial velocities, Galactocentric coordinates, spectral types) for ten new unbound HVS candidates (LAMOST-HVS5 through HVS14). External resources include the Mamajek color-temperature-absolute-magnitude table, LAMOST DR10 data portals, and Gaia/DPAC acknowledgements (the latter removed as generic).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the selection result of high-velocity star candidates by velocity criterion and reliability of Gaia geometric distances.

source_refs.caption: Selection result of 124 high-velocity star candidates
source_refs.label: tab:selection

columns.name: with reliable \tt rgeo
columns.meaning: Number of objects with reliable Gaia geometric distance (rgeo)

columns.name: without reliable \tt rgeo
columns.meaning: Number of objects without reliable rgeo

columns.name: Total number
columns.meaning: Total number of objects/spectra in the category

internal_tables.evidence: Selection result of 124 high-velocity star candidates

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the full catalog of 125 high-velocity candidates with astrometry, spectroscopic classification, kinematics, and quality flags.

columns.name: Name | \nocolhead
columns.meaning: Star name for previously known HVSs or runaways

columns.name: MKCLASS | \nocolhead
columns.meaning: MK spectral classification from the MKCLASS code

columns.name: Vrf | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Radial velocity in the Galactic rest frame

columns.name: Vtot | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Total velocity in the Galactic rest frame

columns.name: dist_source | \nocolhead
columns.meaning: Source of the stellar distance (rgeo, A_index, MK, Brown07, etc.)

columns.name: RV_questionable | \nocolhead
columns.meaning: Flag indicating questionable radial velocity measurement (Y if questionable)

columns.name: Var | \nocolhead
columns.meaning: Flag indicating significant periodic light variation from ZTF (Y if variable)

internal_tables.comments: The complete version of this table is available online.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents detailed multi-band photometry, astrometry, radial and total velocities, Galactocentric coordinates, and spectral types for new HVS candidates LAMOST-HVS5 through HVS8.

external_resources.description: Table of dwarf star colors, effective temperatures, and absolute magnitudes provided by E. Mamajek, used to obtain G-band absolute magnitudes for main-sequence stars.
LAMOST DR10 LRS Line-Index Catalog of A-type Stars, used to derive the Balmer line index - absolute magnitude relation.
LAMOST DR10 AFGK stellar parameter catalog portal, from which improved radial velocities were obtained.
